 {"id":"bd-1tmw5","title":"CAAM: Add --robot-switch-account command","description":"Add robot mode command to manually trigger account switch.

## Command Specification

```bash
ntm --robot-switch-account claude         # Switch to next Claude account
ntm --robot-switch-account openai account3  # Switch to specific account
ntm --robot-switch-account --pane agent-1 claude  # Switch for specific pane
```

## Output Schema (JSON)

```json
{
  "switch": {
    "success": true,
    "provider": "claude",
    "previous_account": "account1@email.com",
    "new_account": "account2@email.com",
    "panes_affected": ["agent-1", "agent-2"],
    "cooldown_seconds": 300
  }
}
```

## Implementation
1. Parse provider and optional account name
2. Call CAAM switch
3. Update affected pane environments
4. Return result JSON

## Code Location
- internal/robot/switch_account.go
- Update: internal/robot/registry.go

## Testing
- Switch to next account, verify output
- Switch to specific account
- Switch with pane filter
- Switch when no accounts available","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:52:03.353964001Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T08:22:05.289374984Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T08:22:05.289301996Z","close_reason":"Implemented --robot-switch-account command for CAAM account switching. Added PrintSwitchAccount in robot package, flag registration in cli/root.go, and unit tests. Supports switching by provider or provider:account with optional pane filter.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1tmw5","depends_on_id":"bd-10qa5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:54:45.210987177Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tmw5","depends_on_id":"bd-1vbqd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:54:40.008135566Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tmw5","depends_on_id":"bd-1yty1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:54:55.084512769Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tmw5","depends_on_id":"bd-26185","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:54:50.866150799Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tmw5","depends_on_id":"bd-8o977","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:52:03.427444617Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Test the spawn --assign convenience flag integration.

## Test Cases

### Flag Parsing
- Parse --assign flag correctly
- Parse --assign with --strategy flag
- Parse --assign with --limit flag
- Error when --assign used without agents

### Integration with ntm assign
- Spawn --assign calls ntm assign internally
- Strategy flag passed through correctly
- Limit flag passed through correctly
- Agent filters applied correctly

### Combined Output
- Output includes spawn result
- Output includes assign result
- JSON format consistent with robot mode

### Error Handling
- Handle assign failure after successful spawn
- Handle no beads available
- Handle all agents busy

## Logging Requirements
- Log spawn phase completion
- Log assign invocation with parameters
- Log combined result

## Test File

 {"id":"bd-1tv6","title":"Task: Implement assignment algorithm","description":"## Purpose
Match beads to agents using capability scores and availability.

## Algorithm
func assignTasks(beads []Bead, agents []Agent, strategy string) []Assignment

For each bead:
1. Filter agents by availability (idle, sufficient context)
2. Score each agent: capability_score * (1 - context_usage)
3. Apply strategy modifiers:
   - balanced: spread work evenly
   - speed: assign to any available
   - quality: assign to highest scorer
   - dependency: prioritize blockers

## Assignment Struct
type Assignment struct {
    Bead       Bead
    Agent      Agent
    Score      float64
    Reason     string
    Confidence float64
}

## Acceptance Criteria
- Optimal assignments based on strategy
- Handles more beads than agents
- Handles more agents than beads
- Provides reasoning for each assignment

## Technical Notes
Consider Hungarian algorithm for optimal matching
Start with greedy approach for simplicity","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:43:21.840916046Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T16:26:37.782032302Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T16:26:37.781936402Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1tv6","depends_on_id":"bd-9tbv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:58:24Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1u462","title":"TOON: Add [robot.output] config section","description":"Add configuration for robot mode output format.

## Config Schema

```toml
[robot.output]
# Default output format: json or toon
format = "json"

# Pretty print output (adds whitespace for readability)
pretty = false

# Include timestamps in output
timestamps = true

# Compression (for large outputs)
compress = false
```

## Implementation Location
- Update: internal/config/config.go
- Update: internal/config/defaults.go

## Struct Definition

```go
type RobotOutputConfig struct {
    Format     string `toml:"format"`  // "json" or "toon"
    Pretty     bool   `toml:"pretty"`
    Timestamps bool   `toml:"timestamps"`
    Compress   bool   `toml:"compress"`
}
```

## Validation
- format must be "json" or "toon"
- If format is "toon", check toon binary available

## Migration
Existing configs without this section default to JSON (backwards compatible).

## Acceptance Criteria

1. RobotOutputConfig struct added to config.go
2. [robot.output] section parsed from ntm.toml
3. Default values: format="json", pretty=false, timestamps=true, compress=false
4. Validation rejects invalid format values with clear error
5. Warning logged if format="toon" but toon binary not found
6. Existing configs without this section work unchanged

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/config/config_test.go
- Test parsing valid config
- Test default values
- Test validation errors

## E2E Test Script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# [E2E-TOON-CONFIG] Robot Output Config Tests
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-TOON-CONFIG]"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Testing [robot.output] config"
# Create temp config with TOON format
mkdir -p /tmp/e2e-toon-test
echo '[robot.output]
format = "json"
pretty = true' > /tmp/e2e-toon-test/ntm.toml
# Verify config is parsed (would need ntm config command)
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Config parsing test requires ntm config command"
```","acceptance_criteria":"1. [robot.output] config section implemented
2. default_format option (json or toon)
3. Respects command-line override
4. Config validation on startup
5.  {"id":"bd-1unv","title":"EPIC: Agent Effectiveness Scoring and Learning","description":"**Agent Effectiveness Scoring and Learning EPIC**

## What It Does
Track agent effectiveness over time and use that data to improve assignments and workflows.

## Why This Matters
- Currently: All agents treated equally
- After: Learn which agents excel at what
- Continuous improvement through data

## Features
- Per-agent success metrics
- Task completion tracking
- Effectiveness scoring algorithm
- Recommendation tuning

## Success Criteria

1. Per-agent metrics tracked and persisted
2. Task completion rates calculated
3. Effectiveness scores computed
4. Scores influence recommendations
5. Dashboard shows agent rankings
6. Robot mode exports scoring data
7. Unit tests for scoring algorithm (80% coverage)
8. E2E tests verify scoring accuracy","notes":"## Testing Requirements

 {"id":"bd-349x","title":"Task: Add webhook configuration in ntm.yaml","description":"## Overview
Implement configuration parsing for webhooks in ntm.yaml.

## Deliverables
1. Create internal/config/webhooks.go:
   - WebhookConfig struct
   - ParseWebhookConfig(yaml []byte) ([]WebhookConfig, error)
   - ValidateConfig() error

2. Config structure:
   webhooks:
     - name: slack-notifications
       url: https://hooks.slack.com/services/xxx
       events:
         - agent.completed
         - agent.error
       formatter: slack
       filter:
         session: myproject*
         agent_type: [claude, codex]
         severity: [warning, error]
       retry:
         max_attempts: 5
         backoff: exponential
       timeout: 30s
       secret: ${NTM_WEBHOOK_SECRET}  # env var substitution

3. Validation rules:
   - URL must be valid HTTP(S)
   - Events must be recognized event types
   - Formatter must be registered
   - Warn on unrecognized options

4. Hot reload:
   - Detect config file changes
   - Reload webhooks without restart
   - Log config changes

## Implementation Notes
- Use mapstructure for YAML parsing
- Support environment variable substitution
- Glob patterns for session filtering

## Acceptance Criteria
- Config parses correctly from ntm.yaml
- Validation catches common errors
- Hot reload works
- Unit tests for config parsing","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:31:50.948504334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T18:32:20.358287058Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-349x","depends_on_id":"bd-2o8r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:32:20.358255358Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-349x","depends_on_id":"bd-3s2f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:31:50.978590620Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3518e","title":"Unit Tests: Dependency awareness and auto-reassignment logic","description":"## Test Coverage
Test the dependency-aware assignment and auto-reassignment on blocker completion.

## Test Cases

### Filtering Blocked Beads
- Filter out beads with non-empty blocked_by
- Include only actionable beads
- Handle bead with multiple blockers
- Handle bead with single blocker

### Unblock Detection
- Detect when blocker bead is closed
- Identify all beads unblocked by completion
- Handle chain unblocking (A blocks B blocks C)
- Handle multiple beads unblocked simultaneously

### Auto-Reassignment
- Queue newly unblocked beads
- Assign to first idle agent
- Respect strategy for reassignment
- Handle no idle agents (queue only)

### Edge Cases
- Circular dependencies (should be filtered)
- Bead completes but no new unblocks
- All agents busy when unblock happens

## Logging Requirements
- Log each filter decision
- Log unblock detection events
- Log reassignment decisions

## Test File
tests/dependency_awareness_test.go","notes":"Created /dp/ntm/tests/dependency_awareness_test.go with 22 comprehensive unit tests:

## Section 1: Filtering Blocked Beads (4 tests)
- TestFilterBlockedBeads_NonEmptyBlockedBy
- TestFilterBlockedBeads_IncludeOnlyActionable  
- TestFilterBlockedBeads_MultipleBlockers
- TestFilterBlockedBeads_SingleBlocker

## Section 2: Unblock Detection (4 tests)
- TestUnblockDetection_BlockerClosed
- TestUnblockDetection_IdentifyAllUnblocked
- TestUnblockDetection_ChainUnblocking (A -> B -> C)
- TestUnblockDetection_MultipleSimultaneous

## Section 3: Auto-Reassignment (4 tests)
- TestAutoReassign_QueueNewlyUnblocked
- TestAutoReassign_AssignToFirstIdle
- TestAutoReassign_RespectStrategy (dependency vs priority)
- TestAutoReassign_NoIdleAgents

## Section 4: Edge Cases (5 tests)
- TestEdgeCase_CircularDependencies
- TestEdgeCase_CompletionNoNewUnblocks
- TestEdgeCase_AllAgentsBusy
- TestEdgeCase_StaleGraph
- TestEdgeCase_PartialBlockerResolution

## Section 5: State Transitions (2 tests)
- TestStateTransition_FailedToAssigned
- TestStateTransition_CompletedCannotRetry

## Section 6: JSON Envelope Format (2 tests)
- TestJSONEnvelope_UnblockedBeadFields
- TestJSONEnvelope_SkippedItemReason

All 22 tests pass.  {"id":"bd-352n","title":"Task: Add help --minimal / --full switch","description":"## Overview
Replace tiered/proficiency gating with a simple help verbosity switch.

## Deliverables
1. Add --minimal and --full flags to ntm help (or ntm --help).
2. Minimal view shows essential commands only (spawn, send, status, kill, help).
3. Full view shows the complete command set (current behavior).
4. Optional config default: help_verbosity = minimal|full.

## Non-Goals
- No proficiency tiers, gating, or promotion logic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Minimal help is concise and stable
- Full help remains unchanged
- No command gating","notes":"Tiered gating removed; this task is the canonical help verbosity switch.

## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

-{"id":"bd-352u","title":"Task: Add timeline persistence for post-session analysis","description":"## Overview
Enable saving timeline data to disk for analysis after session ends.

## Deliverables
1. Create internal/state/timeline_persist.go:
   - SaveTimeline(sessionID string, events []AgentEvent) error
   - LoadTimeline(sessionID string) ([]AgentEvent, error)
   - ListTimelines() ([]TimelineInfo, error)

2. Storage format:
   - JSONL file per session: ~/.local/share/ntm/timelines/<session>.jsonl
   - Each line is one AgentEvent as JSON
   - Include header with session metadata

3. Automatic persistence:
   - Save on session end
   - Periodic checkpoint every 5 minutes
   - Recover from checkpoint on crash

4. CLI commands:
   - ntm timeline show <session-id> - view historical timeline
   - ntm timeline list - list available timelines
   - ntm timeline delete <session-id> - remove old timelines

5. Retention policy:
   - Keep last 30 sessions by default
   - Configurable via ntm.yaml
   - Automatic cleanup of old timelines

## Implementation Notes
- Use standard JSON encoding for portability
- Compress old timelines with gzip
- Index by session ID for fast lookup

## Acceptance Criteria
- Timelines persist across NTM restarts
- Can load and view historical timelines
- Automatic cleanup works
- Unit tests for persistence logic","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:28:33.281526043Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T18:48:36.325029749Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-352u","depends_on_id":"bd-2t7n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:29:00.740423065Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-352u","depends_on_id":"bd-f815","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:28:33.311047947Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-35chh","title":"Test: Unit tests for BeadScanner","description":"## Purpose
Comprehensive unit tests for AllocationCalculator tier calculations, ensuring correct agent allocation based on bead counts.

## Test Structure

```go
// internal/swarm/allocation_test.go
package swarm

import (
    "testing"
    "github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
    "github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestCalculateTier(t *testing.T) {
    cfg := &config.SwarmConfig{
        Tier1Threshold: 400,
        Tier2Threshold: 100,
    }
    calc := NewAllocationCalculator(cfg)
    
    tests := []struct {
        name      string
        beadCount int
        wantTier  int
    }{
        // Tier 1: 400+ beads
        {"tier1_minimum", 400, 1},
        {"tier1_high", 1000, 1},
        {"tier1_exact_boundary", 400, 1},
        
        // Tier 2: 100-399 beads
        {"tier2_minimum", 100, 2},
        {"tier2_maximum", 399, 2},
        {"tier2_middle", 250, 2},
        
        // Tier 3: <100 beads
        {"tier3_maximum", 99, 3},
        {"tier3_minimum", 1, 3},
        {"tier3_zero", 0, 3},
        {"tier3_negative", -1, 3}, // Edge case
    }
    
    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            t.Logf("Testing bead count %d, expecting tier %d", tt.beadCount, tt.wantTier)
            got := calc.CalculateTier(tt.beadCount)
            assert.Equal(t, tt.wantTier, got, 
                "beadCount=%d should be tier %d, got %d", tt.beadCount, tt.wantTier, got)
        })
    }
}

func TestCalculateProjectAllocation(t *testing.T) {
    cfg := &config.SwarmConfig{
        Tier1Threshold: 400,
        Tier2Threshold: 100,
        Tier1Alloc:     config.Allocation{CC: 3, Cod: 2, Gmi: 1},
        Tier2Alloc:     config.Allocation{CC: 2, Cod: 1, Gmi: 1},
        Tier3Alloc:     config.Allocation{CC: 1, Cod: 1, Gmi: 0},
    }
    calc := NewAllocationCalculator(cfg)
    
    tests := []struct {
        name       string
        project    ProjectBeadCount
        wantCC     int
        wantCod    int
        wantGmi    int
        wantTotal  int
    }{
        {
            name:      "tier1_project",
            project:   ProjectBeadCount{Path: "/proj/big", Name: "big", OpenBeads: 500},
            wantCC:    3, wantCod: 2, wantGmi: 1, wantTotal: 6,
        },
        {
            name:      "tier2_project",
            project:   ProjectBeadCount{Path: "/proj/med", Name: "med", OpenBeads: 200},
            wantCC:    2, wantCod: 1, wantGmi: 1, wantTotal: 4,
        },
        {
            name:      "tier3_project",
            project:   ProjectBeadCount{Path: "/proj/small", Name: "small", OpenBeads: 50},
            wantCC:    1, wantCod: 1, wantGmi: 0, wantTotal: 2,
        },
    }
    
    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            t.Logf("Project: %s, Beads: %d", tt.project.Name, tt.project.OpenBeads)
            
            alloc := calc.CalculateProjectAllocation(tt.project)
            
            t.Logf("Result: CC=%d, Cod=%d, Gmi=%d, Total=%d",
                alloc.CCAgents, alloc.CodAgents, alloc.GmiAgents, alloc.TotalAgents)
            
            assert.Equal(t, tt.wantCC, alloc.CCAgents, "CC agents mismatch")
            assert.Equal(t, tt.wantCod, alloc.CodAgents, "Cod agents mismatch")
            assert.Equal(t, tt.wantGmi, alloc.GmiAgents, "Gmi agents mismatch")
            assert.Equal(t, tt.wantTotal, alloc.TotalAgents, "Total agents mismatch")
        })
    }
}

func TestCalculateAllocations_Sorting(t *testing.T) {
    cfg := defaultTestConfig()
    calc := NewAllocationCalculator(cfg)
    
    // Input in random order
    projects := []ProjectBeadCount{
        {Path: "/p/small", Name: "small", OpenBeads: 50},
        {Path: "/p/big", Name: "big", OpenBeads: 500},
        {Path: "/p/med", Name: "med", OpenBeads: 200},
    }
    
    t.Logf("Input order: %v", projectNames(projects))
    
    allocations := calc.CalculateAllocations(projects)
    
    // Should be sorted by bead count descending
    require.Len(t, allocations, 3)
    assert.Equal(t, "big", allocations[0].Project.Name, "First should be highest beads")
    assert.Equal(t, "med", allocations[1].Project.Name, "Second should be medium beads")
    assert.Equal(t, "small", allocations[2].Project.Name, "Third should be lowest beads")
    
    t.Logf("Output order: %v (correctly sorted by bead count desc)", 
        allocationNames(allocations))
}

func TestGenerateSwarmPlan(t *testing.T) {
    cfg := &config.SwarmConfig{
        SessionsPerType: 2,
        // ... full config
    }
    calc := NewAllocationCalculator(cfg)
    
    projects := []ProjectBeadCount{
        {Path: "/p/a", Name: "a", OpenBeads: 450},
        {Path: "/p/b", Name: "b", OpenBeads: 150},
    }
    
    plan := calc.GenerateSwarmPlan("/test", projects)
    
    t.Logf("Plan created: %s", plan.CreatedAt)
    t.Logf("Allocations: %d", len(plan.Allocations))
    t.Logf("Sessions: %d", len(plan.Sessions))
    t.Logf("Totals: CC=%d, Cod=%d, Gmi=%d", plan.TotalCC, plan.TotalCod, plan.TotalGmi)
    
    assert.NotEmpty(t, plan.Sessions, "Should create sessions")
    assert.Equal(t, "/test", plan.ScanDir)
    
    // Verify session naming
    sessionNames := make([]string, 0)
    for _, s := range plan.Sessions {
        sessionNames = append(sessionNames, s.Name)
        t.Logf("Session: %s (type=%s, panes=%d)", s.Name, s.AgentType, s.PaneCount)
    }
    
    assert.Contains(t, sessionNames, "cc_agents_1")
}
```

 {"id":"bd-35vy9","title":"E2E Tests: AutoRespawner full cycle with real tmux","description":"## Purpose
End-to-end tests for AutoRespawner using real tmux sessions to verify the complete respawn cycle works in practice.

## Test Script

```bash
#\!/bin/bash
# e2e_auto_respawner.sh
set -euo pipefail

LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-RESPAWN]"
TEST_SESSION="ntm_e2e_respawn_$$"
PASSED=0
FAILED=0

log() { echo "$LOG_PREFIX $(date +%H:%M:%S) $*"; }
pass() { log "PASS: $1"; ((PASSED++)); }
fail() { log "FAIL: $1"; ((FAILED++)); }

cleanup() {
    log "Cleaning up test session: $TEST_SESSION"
    tmux kill-session -t "$TEST_SESSION" 2>/dev/null || true
}
trap cleanup EXIT

log "Starting AutoRespawner E2E tests"

# Test 1: Create test session and simulate stuck agent
log "Test 1: Graceful agent kill"
tmux new-session -d -s "$TEST_SESSION" -x 120 -y 30
tmux send-keys -t "$TEST_SESSION" "sleep 3600" Enter

sleep 1
log "Agent simulated (sleep process)"

# Attempt graceful kill (Ctrl+C)
tmux send-keys -t "$TEST_SESSION" C-c
sleep 1

# Verify process stopped
PANE_PID=$(tmux display-message -p -t "$TEST_SESSION" "#{pane_pid}")
log "Pane PID: $PANE_PID"

if \! pgrep -P "$PANE_PID" sleep >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    pass "Graceful kill worked"
else
    fail "Graceful kill did not stop process"
fi

# Test 2: Force kill fallback
log "Test 2: Force kill fallback"
tmux send-keys -t "$TEST_SESSION" "trap \"\" SIGINT; sleep 3600" Enter
sleep 1

# Ctrl+C should be ignored
tmux send-keys -t "$TEST_SESSION" C-c
sleep 1

if pgrep -P "$PANE_PID" sleep >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "Process still running (as expected for force kill test)"
    
    # Force kill the child process
    CHILD_PID=$(pgrep -P "$PANE_PID" sleep 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    if [[ -n "$CHILD_PID" ]]; then
        log "Killing child PID: $CHILD_PID"
        kill -9 "$CHILD_PID" 2>/dev/null || true
        sleep 1
        
        if \! pgrep -P "$PANE_PID" sleep >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            pass "Force kill worked"
        else
            fail "Force kill did not stop process"
        fi
    fi
else
    pass "Unexpected: graceful kill worked on trapped process"
fi

# Test 3: Clear pane and respawn
log "Test 3: Clear pane and respawn"
tmux send-keys -t "$TEST_SESSION" "clear" Enter
sleep 0.5
tmux send-keys -t "$TEST_SESSION" "echo AGENT_READY" Enter
sleep 0.5

PANE_OUTPUT=$(tmux capture-pane -t "$TEST_SESSION" -p | tail -5)
log "Pane output: $PANE_OUTPUT"

if echo "$PANE_OUTPUT" | grep -q "AGENT_READY"; then
    pass "Clear and respawn worked"
else
    fail "Clear and respawn failed"
fi

# Test 4: Prompt injection after respawn
log "Test 4: Prompt injection"
PROMPT="echo MARCHING_ORDERS_RECEIVED"
tmux send-keys -t "$TEST_SESSION" "$PROMPT" Enter
sleep 0.5

PANE_OUTPUT=$(tmux capture-pane -t "$TEST_SESSION" -p | tail -5)
if echo "$PANE_OUTPUT" | grep -q "MARCHING_ORDERS_RECEIVED"; then
    pass "Prompt injection worked"
else
    fail "Prompt injection failed"
fi

# Summary
log "=========================================="
log "E2E AutoRespawner Tests Complete"
log "Passed: $PASSED"
log "Failed: $FAILED"
log "=========================================="

if [[ $FAILED -gt 0 ]]; then
    exit 1
fi
exit 0
```

 {"id":"bd-bh74z","title":"Implement hard kill fallback using kill -9","description":"Implement hard kill fallback when soft exit fails.

## When to Use
When soft exit (Ctrl-C) doesn't work after 3 seconds, need to kill -9 the child process.

## Implementation
1. Get shell PID from tmux list-panes
2. Get child PID via pgrep -P <shell_pid>
3. kill -9 <child_pid>
4. Wait for shell to return to prompt

## Code Pattern
pane_pid=$(tmux list-panes -t session:1 -F '#{pane_index} #{pane_pid}' | grep '^N ' | awk '{print $2}')
child_pid=$(pgrep -P $pane_pid | head -1)
kill -9 $child_pid

## Error Handling
- If no child process: already exited, proceed
- If permission denied: return error with diagnostic info
- If shell doesn't return after kill: serious issue, return detailed error","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:25:01.791002696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T02:28:42.240022537Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T02:28:42.239924542Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-bh74z","depends_on_id":"bd-21tqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:25:01.820978166Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

import (

    Providers []string
}

)

func GetStatus() CautStatus {
    once.Do(detect)
    return status
}

func detect() {

    if err != nil {
        return
    }
    // Run 'caut status --json' and parse
    // Populate status struct
}

 {"id":"bd-clpmz","title":"Support external tmux sessions not created by ntm","description":"## Problem

When users create tmux sessions manually (not via `ntm spawn`), ntm commands like `ntm send`, `ntm list`, `ntm status` have issues:
- `ntm send` tries to use pane numbering that assumes ntm's convention (session:N)
- Session metadata is missing (agent types, project mapping)
- Cannot properly target panes by agent type (--cc, --cod, --gmi)

## Example

User creates sessions manually:
```bash
tmux new-session -d -s cc_agents_1
# ... creates 12 panes with cc running in each
```

Then tries to use ntm:
```bash
ntm send cc_agents_1 --cc 'Hello'  # Doesn't work properly
```

## Proposed Solution

1. Add `ntm adopt <session>` command to register external sessions
2. Allow specifying agent type mapping during adoption
3. Store metadata in a way that persists across restarts
4. Make `ntm list` show adopted sessions with a marker
5. Full functionality (send, status, copy, etc.) should work with adopted sessions

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `ntm adopt <session> [--cc=PANES] [--cod=PANES] [--gmi=PANES]` command
- [ ] Adopted sessions appear in `ntm list`
- [ ] `ntm send` works correctly with adopted sessions
- [ ] `ntm status` shows pane details for adopted sessions
- [ ] Documentation updated","notes":"Implemented ntm adopt command to register external tmux sessions for NTM management. Features: pane-to-agent type mapping (--cc, --cod, --gmi, --user), range syntax (0-5), dry-run mode, JSON output support. Added unit tests for parsePaneList.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-22T01:04:51.600762438Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T01:30:48.652599378Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T01:30:48.651537439Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-cqnm8","title":"Implement targeting examples generation","description":"Implement targeting examples generation.

## Fields

- pane_format: 'session:window.pane'
- example_agent_pane: e.g., 'myproject:1.2'
- example_control_pane: e.g., 'myproject:1.1'

## Purpose

Provide copy-paste ready examples for correct pane targeting.
Agents can use these examples as templates.

## Generation Logic

```go
func generateTargetingExamples(session string, windowIdx, ctrlPane, firstAgent int) {
    return TargetingExamples{
        PaneFormat: "session:window.pane",
        ExampleAgentPane: fmt.Sprintf("%s:%d.%d", session, windowIdx, firstAgent),
        ExampleControlPane: fmt.Sprintf("%s:%d.%d", session, windowIdx, ctrlPane),
    }
}
```

- All agent panes list
- Range notation (e.g., 2-16)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:29:49.638785959Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:20:30.029965370Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T05:20:30.029875501Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cqnm8","depends_on_id":"bd-18gwh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:29:49.666986856Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
